rulings.Case Title: CANDACE WATERS VS. JEANNE WOODFORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 20, 2008, line 4 PLAINTIFF CANDACE WATERS (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Expenses. The Court awards attorneys' fees of $1,448,319.60. The award is based on a lodestar of $579,327.84 and a multiplier of 1.5. The Court finds the following billing rates are reasonable:partners $560; senior associates $390; mid-level associates $320; paralegals $150. The Court has reduced the lodestar hours by 10% to address inefficiencies. Given the issues raised by this case and the relief obtained, a multiplier is warranted. The Court also awards costs of $3460.52 for those are recoverable under CCP section 1033.5. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON THOMAS TWELLS DBA RESPONSE-FONE et al VS. SBC BUSINESS COMMUNICATIONS SERVICE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 20, 2008, line 5 DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS A T & T DATACOMM INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. CENTRAL TO THE CLAIM IS WHEN PLAINTIFF'S ACCEPTED THE SYSTEM (FACT 21) THAT IS LINKED TO THE TIME WHEN DEFENDANT NO LONGER WAS OBLIGATED TO REMEDY THE PROBLEMS WITH THE PRODUCT. THE DECLARATION OF MS. DUNHAM-TWELLS CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THIS ISSUE. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION SEEKING A CONTINUANCE ESTABLISHES GOOD CAUSE FOR THE NEED TO DEPOSE MS. PAXSON. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ELOISA ALARCON-MCPEEK VS. ON LOCATION EDUCATION INC., A NEW YORK CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof; Declarations Of Nowland C. Hong And Alan Simon In Support Of Same; (Proposed) Order Compelling Arbitration.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 20, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT ON LOCATION EDUCATION INC., A NEW YORK CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof; Declarations Of Nowland C. Hong And Alan Simon In Support Of Same; (Proposed) Order Compelling Arbitration.Eloise Alarcon-McPeek v. On Location Education Inc The motion to compel arbitration is denied. The seminal decision in California on whether an arbitration provision is enforceable is Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. In determining the enforceability of an arbitration agreement, the California Supreme Court held that the agreement may not limit statutorily imposed remedies and indispensable procedural remedies. (Id. at 103-113) Armendariz requires that (1) all substantive remedies that would be available in a court action be available under the agreement; (2) the parties have adequate opportunity for discovery; (3) the arbitration award is in writing and provides a statement of the supporting rationale; (4) the employee cannot be required to pay unreasonable costs and arbitration fees; and (5) the arbitration must be conducted by a neutral arbitrator. (Id. at 91.) Moreover, where the defendant employer has put in place a mandatory program, its implementation must analyzed based on principles of unconscionability. The analysis starts with a determination of whether this is a contract of adhesion, meaning a contract "imposed and drafted by the party of superior bargaining strength, relegat[ing] to the subscribing party only the opportunity to adhere to the contract or reject it." (Id. at 113) Contracts of adhesion are deemed unenforceable if the terms do not fall within the reasonable expectations of the adhering party and/or the terms are unduly oppressive. (Id.) "[U]nconscionability has both a procedural and substantive element, the former focusing on oppression or surprise due to unequal bargaining power, t

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. RAY BRONNER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 20, 2008, line 9 DEFENDANT RAY BRONNER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES PROTECTED ACTIVITY UNDER CCP 425.16 FOR IT IS BASED ON A COMPLAINT TO THE POLICE AND ACTS TAKEN IN THE COURSE OF LITIGATION. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF HAS A PRIMA FACIA CASE THAT IS NOT BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE CIVIL CODE 47(b) SILBER V ANDERSON (1990) 50 CAL.3RD 205, 215-216. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR THE GRAVERMAN OF CAUSES OF ACTION IS NOT PROTECTED ACTIVITY. MR. BONNER IS DIRECTED TO COMPLY WITH THE RULES OF COURT AS TO THE LENGTH OF BRIEFS. =(302/PJM/VC)
